summary:
Dentinogenesis imperfecta presents with characteristic tooth browning, enamel wear without cavities, pulp obliteration, narrowed canals, and secondary dentin deposition—key findings consistent with the described clinical picture. The family history showing multiple affected male relatives supports an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern.  

\boxed{B}

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
